news.headline: US-Iran Peace Deal Signed — Crude Oil Plunged 5%+ to ~$80/bbl on June 15
news.what_happened: The US and Iran reached an agreement ending their 107-day conflict, committing to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Crude oil crashed 5%+ from above $85, settling near $80.53 on June 15. Crude is recovering slightly to $81.14 on June 16 AM.
news.why_it_matters: India is a major oil importer. Lower crude reduces the import bill, eases inflation, strengthens the rupee, and directly lowers input costs for airlines, paint, and tyre sectors. This is the single biggest macro positive for India in months.

news.headline: S&P 500 +1.65%, Nasdaq +3.07% on June 15 — Wall Street Surges on Iran Deal
news.what_happened: US markets posted their biggest single-day gain in months. S&P 500 closed at 7,554 (+1.65%), Dow added +0.92%, Nasdaq surged +3.07% led by technology stocks reacting to lower energy costs and reduced geopolitical risk.
news.why_it_matters: Strong US close provides a positive global cue for Indian markets. Risk-on environment globally supports FPI flows into India. Nifty has bullish bias entering today, though expiry day adds caution.

news.headline: Rupee Strengthened to 94.75 vs USD — DXY Slips to 99.55 (-0.28%)
news.what_happened: The Indian rupee rallied sharply as the Iran deal improved India macro outlook. USD/INR fell to ~94.75 (rupee at multi-week highs). Dollar Index (DXY) softened to 99.55, down 0.28%.
news.why_it_matters: A stronger rupee is negative for USD-earning IT and pharma exporters, reducing INR value of overseas revenues. However it signals strong macro positioning for India and reduces imported inflation pressure.

news.headline: Nifty Weekly Options Expiry Today — Upper Wick Caution at 24,000
news.what_happened: Nifty closed at 23,856 (+0.95%) on June 15 but left a large upper wick, closing 155 points below the day high of 24,011. June futures trade at 23,930 (73pt premium over spot). Today is Nifty weekly options expiry.
news.why_it_matters: Expiry days bring elevated intraday volatility and whipsaw risk. Resistance cluster at 24,000 may cap upside early. Avoid chasing gap-up opens aggressively; wait for first 15-30 min price discovery.

sector_outlook.reason: Crude -5%+ → jet fuel cost savings, ~40% of airline OPEX
Crude -5%+ → raw material cost reduction for Asian Paints, Berger
Crude -5%+ → petroleum-based input savings for MRF, Apollo, CEAT
Lower crude → reduced inventory gains and marketing margins for HPCL, BPCL, ONGC
Rupee strengthening reduces USD earnings in INR; US rally partially offsets
S&P +1.65%, crude -5%, macro improvement — risk-on globally supports Nifty

picks.catalyst: Crude oil -5%+ on US-Iran peace deal, Strait of Hormuz to reopen
picks.logic: Jet fuel ~40% of IndiGo operating costs. A sustained $20/bbl reduction translates to hundreds of crores in quarterly savings. Iran deal structurally removes the supply-risk premium from crude, improving airline margins for months ahead.

picks.catalyst: Crude -5%+ → significant raw material cost relief
picks.logic: Crude derivatives (monomers, solvents, titanium dioxide feedstocks) comprise 40-50% of paint manufacturing costs. A sustained crude fall directly expands gross margins. Consumer demand intact post Iran-deal; margin recovery likely.

picks.catalyst: Crude -5%+ → petroleum-based raw material cost reduction
picks.logic: Synthetic rubber, carbon black, and other petroleum-derived inputs form 60-70% of tyre raw material costs. Lower crude = direct EBITDA margin expansion. Auto sector sentiment also improves as fuel prices ease for vehicle owners.

risks: Weekly Nifty options expiry today — elevated intraday volatility, avoid chasing gap-up opens blindly
Nifty left upper wick on June 15; resistance at 24,000 may cap early upside
Crude recovering +0.48% on June 16 AM — if reversal accelerates, crude-beneficiary thesis weakens
FII bought only ₹200 Cr cash on June 15 while selling Nifty futures — institutional conviction not fully committed
Iran deal implementation risk — Strait of Hormuz reopening could face delays, reversing crude move

news.headline: WTI Crude Sustains Below $78 — Iran Deal Day 2, Structural Supply Premium Gone
news.what_happened: WTI crude fell >4% on June 16 to ~$77.80/bbl, with Brent near $79.80. Crude has now collapsed ~8-9% from above $85 in just two sessions since the US-Iran MOU signaling Strait of Hormuz reopening. The geopolitical risk premium is being fully unwound.
news.why_it_matters: India imports 85%+ of its oil. Sustained crude at $78 vs $85 last week saves ~$7-8 billion/year in import bill. CAD narrows, inflation eases, and input costs for airlines, tyres, paints, chemicals, and FMCG collapse. Day 2 of the decline means laggard stocks (those that underperformed on Day 1) are the primary opportunity.

news.headline: Dow Hits All-Time Record 52,000; Nasdaq -1.15% on BOJ Rate Hike + New Fed Chair Uncertainty
news.what_happened: A stark divergence in US markets on June 16: Dow surged past 52,000 (record high, +0.64%) while Nasdaq fell 1.15% and S&P 500 was barely flat (-0.08%). Bank of Japan rate hike strengthened the yen. New Fed Chairman Kevin Warsh held his first FOMC meeting, creating policy uncertainty. Tech stocks led the decline.
news.why_it_matters: The Dow/Nasdaq divergence signals a rotation from growth (tech) to value/cyclicals. Indian IT stocks (TCS, INFY, WIPRO, HCLTECH) face direct Nasdaq correlation headwind. Conversely, India's domestic cyclicals — auto, banking, industrials — benefit from the Dow-bullish signal. FIIs may sell tech, rotate to HDFC/ICICI/auto.

news.headline: FII Net Sold Rs 749 Cr on June 16 Even as Nifty Rose for 3rd Straight Day
news.what_happened: Despite Nifty closing at 23,989 (3rd consecutive positive session, Sensex +544 pts), FIIs were net sellers of Rs 749 Cr in cash markets on June 16. DII activity was minimal at Rs 0.06 Cr. The rally was driven by retail/domestic momentum, not institutional conviction.
news.why_it_matters: FII selling even during a rising market suggests smart-money caution — likely driven by BOJ carry trade concerns (stronger yen = FII fund repatriation risk) and USD/INR weakening (rupee fell 0.25% on June 16). This limits how far large-caps can rally today. Watch FII data post-market for trend confirmation.

news.headline: RBI Policy Continuity: Deputy Governor Extended 2 Years; FCNR(B) Hedging Support
news.what_happened: Government extended RBI Deputy Governor Swaminathan J.'s tenure by 2 years from June 26, 2026. RBI is absorbing FCNR(B) hedging costs until September 2026 to attract NRI dollar deposits and support the rupee amid FPI outflows.
news.why_it_matters: Regulatory stability is a key positive for banking sector. The FCNR(B) incentive could draw several billion dollars in NRI inflows, providing rupee support and banking system liquidity. Rate-sensitive sectors benefit from stable, predictable policy environment.

news.headline: Hang Seng -1.17%; China PMI Barely at 50 — Metals Face Demand Headwinds
news.what_happened: Hang Seng fell to 24,552 (-1.17%) on Monday. China's May official manufacturing PMI was at 50.0 (barely expansion, weakest since Feb 2026), with export orders and employment both weakening.
news.why_it_matters: China is the world's largest consumer of steel, aluminium, and copper. Weak PMI and equity market decline signal softening industrial demand. Indian metal producers (TATASTEEL, JSWSTEEL, HINDALCO, SAIL) face a demand-side headwind and should be avoided in today's session.

sector_outlook.reason: WTI crude at $77.80 (-4.1% yesterday, -8-9% in 2 days). Petroleum inputs = 60-70% of tyre costs. APOLLOTYRE moved only +0.06% on June 16 despite the crude crash — clear laggard catchup play today.
Crude low reduces polymer/rubber input costs. Consumer fuel savings boost vehicle purchase sentiment. Dow at record = cyclicals in favor globally. MARUTI, M&M, BAJAJ-AUTO well-positioned.
Jet fuel structurally cheaper. INDIGO had a volatile day June 16 (opened at 4905, closed at 4880 = -0.5%) — consolidation before next leg up expected. Better R/R for new entries now vs yesterday.
Nasdaq -1.15% + BOJ rate hike uncertainty + new Fed Chair policy risk. Rupee marginally weaker (+0.25% USD/INR) provides minor offset but insufficient. Avoid fresh IT longs today.
Hang Seng -1.17% + China PMI at borderline 50 = demand concerns. TATASTEEL, JSWSTEEL, HINDALCO, HINDALCO likely to underperform today.
Crude at $78 compresses HPCL, BPCL, IOC marketing margins. ONGC, OIL see lower per-barrel realization. Structural negative.
Dow at record high, RBI policy continuity, India GDP 7.7% FY26. But FII selling (-Rs 749 Cr) is a headwind. HDFCBANK, ICICIBANK structurally attractive but may face selling pressure intraday.
Crude sustained low = multi-quarter margin tailwind for ASIANPAINT, BERGEPAINT, KANSAINER. Day 2 of crude decline means market still reassessing full margin impact.

picks.catalyst: WTI crude sustained at $77.80 (-4.1% June 16); tyre sector laggard — stock moved only +0.06% on Day 1 of crude collapse
picks.logic: Petroleum-derived inputs (synthetic rubber, carbon black, process oils, naphtha) account for 60-70% of Apollo Tyres' raw material costs. With crude down ~8-9% in two days, quarterly EBITDA margins could expand 200-300 bps on a run-rate basis. The stock barely moved on June 16 (only +0.06% vs the magnitude of the catalyst) — a textbook Day 2 catchup trade. Apollo also has a recovering European business, adding upside to margin expansion.

picks.catalyst: Crude low + Dow record + domestic cyclical rotation; auto is the highest-conviction sector today
picks.logic: MARUTI benefits on three fronts: (1) Petroleum-derived polymer/rubber costs fall directly; (2) Lower petrol prices lift consumer vehicle purchase sentiment — auto loan demand picks up; (3) Dow at record = global value/cyclical preference, and MARUTI is India's premier domestic cyclical with lower FII ownership (less FII-selling pressure than index heavyweights). India GDP at 7.7% and rising exports (16%+ growth Apr-May 2026-27) support urban consumption. Structurally clean balance sheet with no debt.

risks: Iran deal implementation risk: Strait of Hormuz reopening could face delays or political reversals, triggering a crude rebound and collapsing today's entire thesis
FII net selling (Rs 749 Cr June 16) during a rising market signals institutional caution — BOJ carry trade unwinding could accelerate FII outflows from India EMs
Nifty at 23,989 approaching 24,000 psychological resistance; 3 consecutive up days increases profit-booking probability at open
GIFT Nifty +0.20% suggests flat open — if it slips to gap-down at open, momentum reverses quickly in an already cautious institutional environment
Fed Chair Warsh uncertainty: if first FOMC signals hawkishness, DXY strengthens, INR weakens further, FII selling accelerates across EM

news.headline: Fed signals rate hike in 2026 — S&P -1.21%, Nasdaq -1.34%
news.what_happened: Fed Chair Warsh held rates at 3.50-3.75% but 9 of 19 FOMC members pencil in at least one 2026 rate hike. PCE inflation revised to 3.6%. US 10Y yield hit 4.47%.
news.why_it_matters: Hawkish Fed drives FII outflows from EMs. Rising bond yields pressure Indian banks, NBFCs, real estate. Risk-off globally hits Nifty blue chips with high FII ownership.

news.headline: Iran-US peace deal to be formally signed Friday in Switzerland
news.what_happened: US-Iran interim framework agreed: Iran resumes oil exports, Strait of Hormuz reopens, Iran gets access to $300B economic program. Crude up +0.71% today on Trump's residual warning but deal signing is confirmed for Friday.
news.why_it_matters: Iran oil supply returning + Hormuz reopening = significant crude price decline expected. India — world's 3rd-largest oil importer — benefits enormously (CAD narrows, INR stays strong, inflation eases). Airlines, paints, tyres are forward-looking beneficiaries.

news.headline: Gold surges to $4,351/oz (+0.80%) — near all-time record high
news.what_happened: Gold rose 0.80% to $4,351 driven by Fed inflation fears (PCE 3.6%) and residual Iran uncertainty. Silver up +0.89%.
news.why_it_matters: Gold loan NBFCs see immediate benefit — higher gold price = higher collateral value = improved LTV safety, lower NPA risk, stronger loan growth. MUTHOOTFIN is the direct beneficiary.

news.headline: RBI holds repo at 5.25%, trims GDP forecast to 6.6%
news.what_happened: RBI MPC unanimously kept repo unchanged at 5.25% with neutral stance. GDP growth forecast cut to 6.6% for FY27.
news.why_it_matters: No rate-cut catalyst for banks/NBFCs/real estate today. GDP downgrade signals growth caution — mild negative for domestic cyclicals.

news.headline: Rupee strengthens to 94.44 — 1.5% appreciation in one week
news.what_happened: USD/INR fell to 94.44 (from 95.88 on Jun 11), its strongest level in months, driven by Iran deal optimism reducing India's projected oil import bill.
news.why_it_matters: BEARISH for IT exporters (TCS, INFY, WIPRO — USD revenues in INR shrink) and pharma exporters. BULLISH for airlines (dollar-denominated fuel and lease costs cheaper).

sector_outlook.reason: Gold at $4,351 (+0.80%) — record collateral values improve AUM quality and growth
Nasdaq -1.34% + Rupee 1.5% stronger — dual headwind on valuations & USD-INR realization
Rupee at 94.44 compresses export realizations meaningfully
Fed hawkish → FII selling ₹749 Cr; bond yields 4.47%; RBI holds — no NIM catalyst
US 10Y at 4.47% rising; Fed rate hike signal elevates global risk-free rate expectations
Crude +0.71% = headwind today; Iran deal signing Friday → crude to drop significantly
Crude derivatives up today; Iran deal = crude fall next week benefits ASIANPAINT, APOLLOTYRE
China PMI 51.8 (expansion) supports demand; Hang Seng -1.4% and global risk-off cap upside
Risk-off = defensive bid; stocks near 52W lows; but rural demand remains soft
Crude +0.71% boosts realizations today; medium-term bearish post Iran deal signing
Gold at $4,351 drives positive sentiment on jewelry demand and portfolio revaluation

picks.catalyst: Gold at $4,351/oz — near record high, +0.80% overnight on Fed inflation fears
picks.logic: Muthoot Finance is India's largest gold loan NBFC (~₹1 lakh Cr AUM). Higher gold prices expand the collateral value of its entire loan book, improving LTV safety, reducing NPA risk, and enabling higher loan disbursals per gram. At $4,351, the collateral headroom is at multi-month highs — positive for both AUM growth and asset quality. In a risk-off session with gold as safe-haven, MUTHOOTFIN typically outperforms the broader market. Direct, immediate P&L linkage. Risk-reward: +3% target vs -1.5% stop = 2:1.

risks: Weekly options expiry today (Thursday) — elevated intraday volatility; avoid chasing gap-ups >0.5%
FII sold ₹749 Cr on Jun 17; continuation of selling pressures Nifty 50 blue chips
Trump Iran warnings could delay Friday signing → crude spike → broader inflation risk
US 10Y at 4.47% rising — if crosses 4.60%, global risk-off intensifies significantly
RBI GDP forecast cut to 6.6% signals domestic growth caution

news.headline: US-Iran Peace Deal Formally Signed — Crude Oil Extends Decline to $74.62 (WTI)
news.what_happened: The US and Iran digitally finalized their interim peace agreement, raising expectations of the Strait of Hormuz reopening for commercial shipping. WTI crude fell to $74.62/bbl and Brent to ~$77.80, continuing the rout from $85+ levels before the deal. Cumulative crude drop since June 14 exceeds 9%.
news.why_it_matters: India imports ~85% of its crude needs — every $5/bbl drop in crude narrows the current account deficit by ~$6-7B annually and eases retail inflation. Jet fuel, synthetic rubber, carbon black, and petrochemical feedstocks all get cheaper. Direct P&L tailwind for airlines, paints, tyres, chemicals, and logistics. Rupee also strengthens (94.40 from 95.88 peak on June 11).

news.headline: US Fed Holds Rates Unchanged — S&P 500 +1.08%, Nasdaq +1.91% on June 18
news.what_happened: The Federal Reserve kept the benchmark rate unchanged at its June meeting. Though the Fed signaled growing hawkish tilt (~half of policymakers favor a hike by Oct 2026), markets viewed the hold as a short-term reprieve. S&P 500 closed at 7,500.58 (+1.08%), Nasdaq at 26,517.93 (+1.91%). Chip stocks led. US markets are CLOSED today (Juneteenth holiday).
news.why_it_matters: Global risk-on lifts FII sentiment toward EM markets including India. Indian IT stocks sold off -1 to -2% on June 18 morning on hawkish fears — with Nasdaq now +1.91%, IT recovery is likely. FII flows expected positive. US closed today = no incremental cue, but the June 18 close sets a strong backdrop.

news.headline: RBI Keeps Repo Rate at 5.25%, Neutral Stance; India Removes Tax on Foreign Bond Investment
news.what_happened: RBI left repo rate unchanged at 5.25% in its latest meeting, maintaining neutral stance amid cautious inflation outlook. Separately, the Centre announced removal of tax on interest income and capital gains from government securities for foreign investors — India's biggest bond market reform in years.
news.why_it_matters: Rate stability anchors NIM expectations for banks. The bond reform could attract $10-15B in fresh FII flows over coming quarters, supporting the rupee and reducing G-Sec yields — bullish for NBFCs and real estate via lower cost of capital. Short-term liquidity impact on bond markets positive.

news.headline: Nikkei +1.8% Crosses 71,000 for First Time; Asia Risk-On (China/HK Closed for Dragon Boat Festival)
news.what_happened: Japan's Nikkei 225 surged ~1.8% to cross 71,000 for the first time — boosted by the US-Iran peace deal and Nasdaq's surge. Korea's KOSPI also hit record 9,000. Hong Kong and mainland China markets are CLOSED today for Dragon Boat Festival holiday.
news.why_it_matters: Regional risk-on supports FII flows into India. Absence of China/HK markets eliminates a potential drag. Nikkei at record high signals strong global sentiment. Positive backdrop for broad Nifty rally, especially large-cap bluechips with high FII ownership.

news.headline: India Merchandise Exports Up 16.09% YoY in Apr-May FY27 to $88.91 Billion
news.what_happened: India's merchandise exports for April-May FY2026-27 rose 16.09% YoY to $88.91 billion, driven by manufacturing, chemicals, and electronics. This comes even as the rupee has remained structurally weak at 94-96 per dollar.
news.why_it_matters: Strong export performance validates Make-in-India manufacturing momentum and signals competitive pricing of Indian goods globally. The weak rupee has actually aided export competitiveness. Positive macro signal — reduces pressure on trade balance alongside the crude fall.

sector_outlook.reason: WTI crude at $74.62 (-2.5%) — jet fuel is 35-40% of OPEX; massive cost relief
Crude derivatives (40-50% of raw material) cheaper; margin expansion expected
Synthetic rubber and carbon black (60-70% input) linked to crude; tailwind
Fuel = key OPEX; crude drop directly improves margins
Lower fuel prices lift consumer vehicle demand; cheaper logistics
Nasdaq +1.91% positive but rupee strengthened (94.4 from 95.88) offsets USD revenue gains
US risk-on + FII buying + RBI stable rate + India bond reform = positive flows
Crude down = cheaper packaging/plastics; rupee strengthening eases import costs
ONGC/OIL — lower crude realization; HPCL/BPCL/IOC — lower inventory gains
China/HK closed (Dragon Boat Festival) — no fresh demand cue from largest buyer
No sector-specific trigger; rupee strength mild headwind for exporters
Rate unchanged (no cut) but bond reform positive; construction costs easing with crude
Gold at $4,188 (fell from $4,210) — risk-on reduces safe-haven demand; stable collateral

picks.catalyst: WTI crude -2.5% to $74.62; Brent ~$77.80 on US-Iran peace deal progress
picks.logic: Jet fuel constitutes ~38-40% of IndiGo's total OPEX. Every $1/bbl fall in crude saves IndiGo approximately ₹400-450 Cr annually. With crude down >$10 from pre-war levels (was $85+), this is a ₹4,000-4,500 Cr annual earnings tailwind — transformational for airline profitability. IndiGo also benefits from lower lease costs as global aircraft demand stabilizes. Strong domestic passenger traffic growth reinforces the bullish case.

picks.catalyst: Crude and petrochemical feedstocks cheaper; domestic consumption stable
picks.logic: Crude oil derivatives (titanium dioxide, VAM, ethylene oxide) constitute 40-50% of Asian Paints' raw material basket. With WTI at $74.62 (down from $85+ in May 2026), input costs are set for meaningful relief in Q1/Q2 FY27. Asian Paints already trades at a premium to history on volume recovery expectations — margin expansion from lower raws is a strong re-rating catalyst. Management had guided for margin improvement if crude sustains below $80.

picks.catalyst: Crude-linked synthetic rubber and carbon black prices falling sharply
picks.logic: 60-70% of Apollo Tyres' raw material cost is linked to crude: synthetic rubber (SBR, BR), carbon black, nylon fabric, and process oils all derived from petrochemicals. With WTI at $74.62 (down ~$10+ from May highs), Apollo's input cost basket will deflate substantially in Q1/Q2 FY27. Apollo also has European operations where energy cost relief is amplified. Stock trades at reasonable 12-13x FY27E earnings — the crude tailwind can lift EPS estimates meaningfully.

risks: GIFT Nifty at 24,003 vs Nifty spot close of 24,168 (-165 pts) indicates cautious/lower open — watch 24,000 as key support; breach = step aside
US markets CLOSED today (Juneteenth holiday) — no real-time US cues; global liquidity thinner than usual
Post-Thursday-expiry Friday — options OI unwinding can cause sharp intraday swings; use tight stops
Fed hawkish signal (rate hike priced for Oct 2026, US 10Y at 4.46%) — any strong US data could trigger FII outflows from EM next week
Strait of Hormuz reopening timeline uncertain — if implementation stalls, crude can rebound sharply, reversing all crude-sensitive picks
INDIGO/ASIANPAINT/APOLLOTYRE may gap-up at open (crude story is well-known) — avoid chasing; only enter in watch zone
Nifty at 5th consecutive gain — increased profit-taking risk intraday
notes: US markets closed (Juneteenth). China/HK closed (Dragon Boat Festival). Not an expiry day (weekly expiry was yesterday, June 18 Thursday). GIFT Nifty -0.68% vs Nifty spot suggests mild caution despite strong US/Asia backdrop. Dominant theme: US-Iran peace deal = crude crash = broad India bullish.

news.headline: Jio Platforms files DRHP for IPO + Reliance announces $110B AI investment
news.what_happened: Reliance Industries' subsidiary Jio Platforms filed DRHP (Draft Red Herring Prospectus) with SEBI for a public listing. Separately, Reliance announced a $110 billion investment in AI across telecom, healthcare, education and agriculture verticals.
news.why_it_matters: Jio IPO will unlock standalone valuation (~$100B+) for Reliance shareholders. Friday's Sensex was dragged by IT weakness, so Reliance's own catalysts were overshadowed. Monday is the first full session where the market can fully price in both Jio IPO and AI investment news.

news.headline: Crude oil down 17.42% in past month — Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire + US-Iran talks ease supply risk
news.what_happened: WTI crude is at $77.54/bbl, down ~17.42% over the past month after Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire agreement and ongoing US-Iran nuclear talks in Switzerland. Crude saw a ~8% weekly decline last week on ceasefire news. Today's +0.27% is a minor consolidation.
news.why_it_matters: India's aviation, paints, tyres and logistics sectors have crude as a dominant cost driver. A 17.42% monthly crude decline directly compresses these sectors' largest input cost lines. IndiGo saves ~₹400 Cr per $1 crude decline annually. Asian Paints' raw material basket falls 40-50% in correlation with crude.

news.headline: US markets closed strong on Thursday (S&P +1.08%, Nasdaq +1.91%) before Juneteenth holiday
news.what_happened: US markets closed higher on Thursday June 18 with S&P 500 at 7,500.58 (+1.08%) and Nasdaq at 26,517.93 (+1.91%), driven by tech recovery and optimism over US-Iran deal progress. Markets were closed Friday June 19 (Juneteenth). Monday June 22 is the first US market open day.
news.why_it_matters: Strong US close signals global risk-on, supporting FII flows into India. Nifty blue chips with high FII ownership (HDFCBANK, RELIANCE, ICICIBANK) benefit. Nasdaq +1.91% also provides partial counter to Accenture-driven IT selling from Friday.

news.headline: IT sector hammered Friday — Infosys -6%, TCS -3.5% on Accenture revenue guidance cut
news.what_happened: Indian IT stocks sold off sharply on June 19: Infosys -6%, TCS -3.5%, HCL Tech -2.7%, Tech Mahindra -2.5%. Trigger was Accenture cutting its FY26 revenue growth guidance, signaling slowing enterprise IT spending globally.
news.why_it_matters: Accenture is a leading indicator for Indian IT — same client base. Signals discretionary IT spending slowdown in global markets. However, Friday's sharp decline may be partially oversold. Rupee at 94.46/USD (5%+ YTD depreciation) provides earnings buffer. Watch for bounce vs. continued sell.

news.headline: RBI FCNR(B) forex swap facility reporting deadline today; India removes tax on foreign bond investors
news.what_happened: RBI's FCNR(B) forex swap facility (launched June 8) requires first submission from banks by June 22 today. Banks can now offer up to 8% on NRI deposits till Sep 2026. Centre also removed taxes on interest and capital gains from Indian government securities for foreign investors, potentially attracting $40B+.
news.why_it_matters: Positive for rupee stabilization and banking sector capital. Bond market reform could attract record FPI inflows into G-Secs. Potentially bearish for IT/Pharma exporters if rupee appreciates, but bullish for banks and rate-sensitive sectors in the medium term.

news.headline: India-EU FTA finalized — 93% of Indian exports get duty-free EU access
news.what_happened: Under the India-EU Free Trade Agreement, 93% of Indian shipments will enjoy duty-free access to the 27-nation EU bloc. Luxury EU imports like cars and wines will become cheaper in India.
news.why_it_matters: Medium-term structural positive for Indian exporters across auto components, engineering, textiles and pharma. EU is India's second-largest trading partner. Sentiment boost today for export-oriented manufacturing names.

sector_outlook.reason: Jio Platforms DRHP filed; $110B AI investment — two major catalysts converge
Crude down 17.42% monthly — massive jet fuel cost relief for IndiGo
Crude monthly decline reduces petroleum-derived raw material costs 40-50%
FII net buyers ₹4,859 Cr; US markets strong; RBI bond reforms boost capital inflows
Bharat Forge wins ₹425 Cr MoD contract; sustained Govt defense capex cycle
Rate cut expectations; foreign bond market reforms improve long-term financing
Accenture guidance cut = sector headwind; but Nasdaq +1.91% + weak rupee (94.46) = countervailing support
Crude benefit on packaging costs; El Niño monsoon risk threatens rural demand
China GDP 4.5-5% stable; Hang Seng -1.59% — no strong catalyst either way
Crude monthly -17.42% hurts ONGC realization; OMC face inventory losses + fuel price cut risk

picks.catalyst: Jio Platforms DRHP filed for IPO; Reliance announces $110B AI investment
picks.logic: Jio Platforms is India's largest telecom company and a potential top-5 global IPO. DRHP filing triggers institutional repricing of Jio's standalone value (~$100B+). Separately, the $110B AI investment signals a platform-company transformation. Friday's Sensex was dragged by IT weakness — Reliance's own catalysts were overshadowed. Monday opens fresh. Stock at ₹1,309.50 is well below 52-week high of ₹1,611 — room to run. FII buying ₹4,859 Cr and GIFT Nifty +0.58% add broad tailwind.

picks.catalyst: Crude oil down 17.42% monthly — jet fuel is 35-40% of IndiGo OPEX
picks.logic: IndiGo (India's largest airline by market share >60%) sees its biggest cost line — jet fuel — fall dramatically as crude drops 17.42% month-on-month. Every $1 drop in crude saves ~₹400 Cr annually. At $77.54 vs ~$94 a month ago, the saving is substantial. GIFT Nifty positive, strong FII buying, and Nifty blue chip status add to the trade. Stock at ₹5,029 with 52-week range context offering momentum upside.

picks.catalyst: FII net buyers ₹4,859 Cr Friday + US markets strong (S&P +1.08%, Nasdaq +1.91%)
picks.logic: HDFCBANK is India's most FII-owned private sector bank and the most liquid way to play a global risk-on trade. US markets closed at highs Thursday, Nasdaq up 1.91%, and FIIs were net buyers Friday. RBI bond market reforms and FCNR(B) swap facility to attract $40B+ in foreign capital flow = bullish for the banking sector. Stock at ₹779.80 is significantly below 52-week high of ₹1,020 — providing substantial upside room. Rate cut cycle still in progress, expanding NIM outlook.

risks: IT sector (Infosys -6% on Friday) carries high Nifty 50 weight — if selling continues, index gains are capped despite positive broad tone
Crude oil +0.27% today — if Strait of Hormuz tension spikes or Iran talks collapse, crude rebounds sharply and reverses Airlines/Paints tailwinds
GIFT Nifty at 24,153 signals ~0.58% gap-up open — if gap-up exceeds 1%, watch zones shift higher; reduce lot size on gapping stocks
Rupee at 94.46/USD — RBI success in stabilizing rupee via FCNR(B) could cause appreciation, headwind for IT/Pharma exporters

news.headline: US-Iran 60-Day Peace Roadmap → WTI Crude -2.23% to $74.07
news.what_happened: The US Treasury authorised the production, delivery and sale of Iranian oil and petroleum products for 60 days. The US and Iran agreed on a roadmap toward a final nuclear deal, boosting expectations of gradual Hormuz Strait supply recovery. WTI crude fell to $74.07, its lowest since early March.
news.why_it_matters: Crude -2.23% directly slashes fuel & raw-material costs for India's oil-dependent sectors. IndiGo saves ~₹350-400 Cr/year per $1 crude drop. Tyre makers (synthetic rubber, carbon black = 60-70% of RM) and paint companies (crude derivatives = 40-50% of RM) get immediate margin expansion. India's CAD also narrows, supporting the rupee.

news.headline: US Nasdaq +3.07%, S&P 500 +1.65% — Dow Hits Record 51,671
news.what_happened: US equity markets surged on June 22 as the Iran deal eased inflation fears. Nasdaq posted its biggest single-session gain in months (+3.07%), the S&P 500 rose 1.65%, and the Dow Jones hit a new record close of 51,671. Oil price relief is seen as a structural inflation-fighter.
news.why_it_matters: Indian IT stocks closely shadow Nasdaq moves. A 3%+ Nasdaq day historically translates to 1.5-2.5% IT sector move in India next session. USD/INR at 94.25 (rupee structurally weak vs history) means USD-revenue earners report outsized INR revenue. FII flows likely to continue into Indian large-caps.

news.headline: FII Net Buying ₹4,859 Cr on June 22 — 4th Consecutive Day of Inflows
news.what_happened: Foreign institutional investors were net buyers of ₹4,859 Cr in the cash segment on June 22. DIIs were net sellers of ₹1,160 Cr providing liquidity. This is the fourth consecutive session of meaningful FII inflows.
news.why_it_matters: Sustained FII buying lifts index heavyweights with high FII ownership (HDFCBANK, RELIANCE, ICICIBANK, INFY, TCS). Broad market breadth improves. Signals global risk-on rotation into India as an EM beneficiary of lower crude and stable macro.

news.headline: RBI Holds Repo Rate at 5.25%; Says May Inflation Pickup is Transient
news.what_happened: The RBI kept the repo rate unchanged at 5.25% for the third consecutive meeting, maintaining a neutral stance. RBI's June bulletin stated inflation remains anchored below target despite the recent energy shock. GDP forecast for FY27 lowered to 6.6% from 6.9%.
news.why_it_matters: No near-term rate-hike risk from RBI removes a key overhang on rate-sensitive sectors. Bond yields may ease slightly, benefiting banks' treasury portfolios. NBFCs and housing finance see continued cheap-liquidity environment. However, RBI neutral stance (not dovish) limits any fresh upside catalyst for banks.

news.headline: Nikkei +1.58% to 72,379; Shanghai +1.8% — Asia Risk-On Broad
news.what_happened: Asian markets rallied strongly on June 23 morning: Nikkei 225 gained 1.58% to close at 72,379 — among its highest levels. Shanghai Composite rose 1.8%. Taiwan Taiex surged 2.7% to a record intraday high. Hang Seng lagged (-0.3%) after a technical death cross.
news.why_it_matters: Broad Asia risk-on supports FII sentiment toward Indian equities. Strong Chinese industrial momentum (PMI 51.8 in May) is positive for metals demand. Nikkei strength reflects global manufacturing recovery, a positive backdrop for India's capex and industrial cycle.

picks.catalyst: WTI Crude -2.23% overnight on US-Iran 60-day peace roadmap
picks.logic: Jet fuel constitutes 35-40% of IndiGo's total operating costs. At ~$74/bbl WTI, fuel is well below IndiGo's budget assumptions. Every $1 crude drop saves IndiGo ~₹350-400 Cr/year in fuel. With Iran deal reducing geopolitical risk premium, crude may remain suppressed for weeks. IndiGo commands ~60% domestic market share and has strong operational leverage. Direct P&L benefit is immediate and significant.

picks.catalyst: Nasdaq +3.07% on June 22; USD/INR at 94.25 (structurally weak rupee)
picks.logic: Infosys derives ~85% of revenues from USD. Nasdaq's 3.07% surge — largest single-day gain in months — drives valuation re-rating for Indian IT. Infosys already gained 1.29% on June 22; momentum building. USD/INR at 94.25 vs historical ~83-84 means every USD dollar of revenue is worth ~13% more in INR. FII inflows (₹4,859 Cr) directly support high-FII-ownership stocks like INFY. Q1 FY27 results season beginning — strong deal pipeline expected.

picks.catalyst: WTI Crude -2.23%; synthetic rubber and carbon black (crude derivatives) = 60-70% of RM cost
picks.logic: Apollo Tyres sources synthetic rubber and carbon black — both petroleum-derived — as 60-70% of raw material costs. Crude at $74 (vs ~$92 in Jan 2026) means RM basket is significantly cheaper. Every 5% crude fall improves Apollo's EBITDA margin by ~150-200bps. European operations add a USD/EUR revenue layer. At ₹425.50, stock is near lower end of its 52W range (₹365–₹540) offering asymmetric upside. Crude catalyst is more structural (Iran deal) not just a one-day blip.

risks: If Nifty gaps up >1% at open (GIFT Nifty suggesting ~0.74%), watch zones for all picks shift — reduce size by 50% on gap-opens
US-Iran deal is a 60-day roadmap, not a finalized agreement — any breakdown reverses crude gains sharply and punishes airlines/tyres
Fed's hawkish tilt (9/19 members favour rate hike in late 2026) — dollar strength risk could trigger FII reversal in coming sessions
Hang Seng death cross is a technical warning — if HSI breaks down, FII risk-off could spread to India metals and broader market
RBI inflation outlook upgraded to 5.1% — limits scope for rate cuts; NBFCs/real estate miss a catalyst
Not an expiry day (Thursday = weekly options expiry) — normal volatility expected; no elevated gamma risk today
